memes.label: Ambition
memes.description: "Ambition as a motive force in Trollope's Rachel Ray, driving characters to seek social advancement, romantic fulfillment, commercial success, and spiritual influence—and the moral scrutiny to which such ambitions are subjected"

memes.label: Baslehurst
memes.description: The provincial Devonshire town that provides the social world of Trollope's novel—a community of breweries, churches, gossip, and elections where everyone knows everyone else's business

memes.label: Baslehurst Church
memes.description: The parish church of Baslehurst where Mr. Comfort preaches his moderate Anglicanism and where Sunday services become a theater of social observation and spiritual scrutiny

memes.label: The Baslehurst Election
memes.description: The parliamentary election that forms the novel's political subplot, drawing Baslehurst into the wider currents of Victorian public life and testing the political loyalties of its citizens

memes.label: The Baslehurst Gazette
memes.description: The local newspaper of Baslehurst that reports on town affairs, brewery news, and election proceedings—representing the institutional dimension of provincial public opinion

memes.label: Baslehurst Inn
memes.description: The public house in Baslehurst that serves as a gathering place for townsmen and a neutral ground for conversations that cross social boundaries

memes.label: Bragg's End
memes.description: The modest cottage outside Baslehurst where Mrs. Ray, Mrs. Prime, and Rachel Ray live—a household governed by Evangelical piety and maternal anxiety

memes.label: Brewery
memes.description: "Bungall and Tappitt's brewery in Baslehurst as the novel's commercial setting, site of romantic conflict, and symbol of generational change in Rachel Ray"

memes.label: Bungall & Tappitt
memes.description: The brewing partnership in Baslehurst founded by the late Mr. Bungall and operated by Mr. Tappitt, whose disputed succession drives the novel's commercial subplot

memes.label: Bungall & Tappitt's Brewery
memes.description: The commercial heart of Baslehurst, where Mr. Tappitt presides over the brewing of beer and Luke Rowan arrives to claim his partnership—setting the novel's business plot in motion

memes.label: Cawston
memes.description: A rural parish in Devonshire near Baslehurst, home to Bragg's End Cottage and site of Cawston Bridge, where key encounters between Rachel Ray and Luke Rowan take place.

memes.label: Cawston Bridge
memes.description: A locality near Baslehurst that features in the novel's election subplot, representing the geographical extension of provincial political life beyond the town center

memes.label: Cherry Tappitt
memes.description: The youngest and most independent of the three Tappitt sisters, whose loyalty to Luke Rowan and Rachel Ray persists even when her family turns against them

memes.label: Church
memes.description: "The Church of England as an institutional and spiritual force in Trollope's Rachel Ray, shaping the moral universe of Baslehurst through the parsonage, evangelical zeal, and the competing claims of Low Church and Broad Church sympathies"

memes.label: Social Class
memes.description: "Social class distinctions and their influence on romantic choices, business relations, and community dynamics in Trollope's Rachel Ray"

memes.label: The Cornbury Estate
memes.description: The landed estate of the Cornbury family, representing the institutional dimension of the gentry's social authority and electoral influence in the Baslehurst district

memes.label: Cornbury Grange
memes.description: The country seat of the Cornbury family near Baslehurst, representing the benevolent potential of the landed gentry in Trollope's provincial England

memes.label: The Counting-house
memes.description: The administrative office of Bungall & Tappitt's brewery, where accounts are kept and commercial authority is exercised—a masculine domain of ledgers and contracts

memes.label: Dorcas Society
memes.description: The charitable sewing circle in Baslehurst where pious ladies gather to make garments for the poor, providing Mrs. Prime with a socially approved outlet for her Evangelical energies

memes.label: The Dorcas Society
memes.description: The charitable sewing circle of Baslehurst—a female organization dedicated to making garments for the poor and providing pious women like Mrs. Prime with an approved outlet for energy and influence

memes.label: Dr. Harford
memes.description: The physician of Baslehurst whose quiet decency and professional standing make him one of the community's solid citizens and an occasional counselor in family matters

memes.label: Duty
memes.description: "The concept of duty as a moral framework governing character decisions in Rachel Ray, encompassing filial obligation, religious devotion, and social responsibility"

memes.label: Election
memes.description: "The parliamentary election at Baslehurst as a political subplot in Rachel Ray, intersecting with the novel's romantic and commercial conflicts"

memes.label: Exeter
memes.description: The cathedral city and county town of Devonshire, mentioned as a point of reference for legal business, social connections, and the wider world beyond Baslehurst

memes.label: Family
memes.description: "Family as the fundamental social unit in Trollope's Rachel Ray, encompassing the Ray household, the Tappitt brewing dynasty, and the networks of kinship that shape marriage, inheritance, and social standing in Victorian Baslehurst"

memes.label: Gentleman
memes.description: "The contested ideal of the 'gentleman' in Trollope's Rachel Ray—a marker of social rank, moral worth, and personal conduct that characters invoke to claim status, judge suitors, and navigate the class boundaries of provincial Victorian life"

memes.label: Gossip
memes.description: "Gossip as a form of social communication and control in Trollope's Rachel Ray, mediating reputation, courtship, and community judgment in the confined world of Baslehurst"

memes.label: Jealousy
memes.description: "Jealousy as a destructive social emotion in Trollope's Rachel Ray, examined through the Tappitt family's resentment of Luke Rowan, romantic rivalry, and the petty envy that animates provincial life in Baslehurst"

memes.label: London
memes.description: The distant metropolis from which Luke Rowan comes and to which the novel's provincial world is implicitly contrasted—a place of wider opportunities and sharper commercial instincts

memes.label: Love
memes.description: "Love as romantic attachment, familial affection, and religious devotion in Trollope's Rachel Ray, explored through courtship, marriage, and the contrasting emotional lives of the Ray family women"

memes.label: Luke Rowan
memes.description: The young London brewer who arrives in Baslehurst to claim his partnership in Bungall & Tappitt's brewery and wins the heart of Rachel Ray

memes.label: Marriage
memes.description: "The social institution of marriage as a central theme in Anthony Trollope's Rachel Ray, shaping character motivations, social standing, and the novel's romantic plot"

memes.label: Martha Tappitt
memes.description: Eldest daughter of the Tappitt brewing family in Baslehurst, known for her honesty, justice, and sagacious practicality.

memes.label: Mary Rowan
memes.description: Luke Rowan's younger sister, who accompanies her brother to Mrs. Tappitt's ball and provides Rachel Ray with a glimpse of the London world from which Luke comes

memes.label: Miss Harford
memes.description: The rector's daughter in Baslehurst, a good-natured minor character who defends Rachel Ray against Tappitt family gossip.

memes.label: Miss Pucker
memes.description: A comic spinster of Baslehurst whose sharp tongue and sharper curiosity make her the town's most reliable conduit of gossip, particularly regarding Rachel Ray's romantic affairs

memes.label: Money
memes.description: "Money as an economic and moral force in Trollope's Rachel Ray, mediating marriage prospects, business success, and social standing in the provincial world of Baslehurst"

memes.label: Mr. Comfort
memes.description: The vicar of Baslehurst who combines clerical office with legal training, serving as a pragmatic mediator in both the Ray family's domestic conflicts and the Tappitt-Rowan brewery dispute

memes.label: Mr. Goodall
memes.description: Mrs. Ray's financial advisor in Baslehurst, who manages the sale of her property and the investment of the proceeds after Mr. Ray's death.

memes.label: Mr. Griggs
memes.description: A comic suitor at the Tappitt ball, known for his peculiar jewellery and awkward courtship of the younger women, who becomes an object of ridicule among the party guests.

memes.label: Mr. Hart
memes.description: A wealthy cloth merchant from Hound Street and Liberal parliamentary candidate for Baslehurst, whose election campaign becomes entangled with the Tappitt brewery dispute.

memes.label: Mr. Honyman
memes.description: The Tappitt family's attorney in Baslehurst, who advises on the brewery partnership dispute between Mr. Tappitt and Luke Rowan.

memes.label: Mr. Prong
memes.description: The stern Evangelical curate of Baslehurst whose severe sermons attract Mrs. Prime's admiration and whose personal interest in her creates an unexpected romantic subplot

memes.label: Mr. Sharpit
memes.description: An attorney in Baslehurst who meets Mr. Tappitt at the Dragon Inn to negotiate the brewery dispute, described as a dangerous companion with a taste for gin and bitters.

memes.label: Mr. Sturt
memes.description: A tenant farmer on the Cornbury estate at Bragg's End, neighbour to Mrs. Ray and Rachel Ray, whose steady goodwill and quiet respect for Rachel's choices embody the novel's rural moral centre

memes.label: Mr. Tappitt
memes.description: The aging proprietor of Bungall & Tappitt's brewery in Baslehurst, who resists the young Luke Rowan's claim to partnership while managing his three marriageable daughters

memes.label: Mrs. Butler Cornbury
memes.description: The wife of Squire Cornbury of Cornbury Grange, whose social patronage transforms Rachel Ray from a provincial nobody into the belle of Mrs. Tappitt's ball

memes.label: Mrs. Prime
memes.description: Rachel Ray's widowed elder sister, a stern Evangelical whose religious severity dominates the Bragg's End household and opposes Rachel's romance with Luke Rowan

memes.label: Mrs. Ray
memes.description: The gentle, timorous widow of Bragg's End cottage, mother of Rachel and Dorothea, whose "weak, vacillating" nature is constantly torn between her two daughters' opposing temperaments

memes.label: Mrs. Rowan
memes.description: Luke Rowan's mother, a London widow who opposes her son's marriage to the portionless Rachel Ray on social grounds, representing the worldly wisdom that the novel treats with measured skepticism

memes.label: Mrs. Rule
memes.description: An elderly widow of Baslehurst whose mild, harmless nature provides protective chaperonage to Rachel Ray at the Tappitt ball

memes.label: Mrs. Sturt
memes.description: A farmer's wife and warm-hearted neighbour to the Ray family at Bragg's End, who offers practical kindness, folk wisdom, and maternal comfort to Rachel Ray throughout her romantic trials.

memes.label: Mrs. Tappitt
memes.description: The socially ambitious wife of the Baslehurst brewer, whose schemes for her three daughters' marriages make her Rachel Ray's most persistent antagonist in the novel's comedy of provincial manners

memes.label: Mrs. Tappitt's Ball
memes.description: The major social event of Baslehurst's season, hosted by the Tappitts at the brewery, where Rachel Ray is declared "the belle of the evening"(008-002) and the novel's romantic and social tensions converge

memes.label: Neighbourhood
memes.description: The social fabric of proximity in a Victorian country town — how physical nearness shapes the moral obligations, gossip networks, and community judgments that drive the novel's domestic drama.

memes.label: Pride
memes.description: "Pride as a moral and psychological force in Trollope's Rachel Ray, examined through the self-regard of characters who navigate social ambition, romantic vulnerability, and the contested boundary between proper self-respect and destructive vanity"

memes.label: Propriety
memes.description: "Propriety as a governing social code in Trollope's Rachel Ray, regulating courtship, speech, and female behavior in the provincial world of Baslehurst"

memes.label: Rachel Ray
memes.description: The eponymous heroine of Anthony Trollope's *Rachel Ray*, a fair-haired young woman of Baslehurst caught between Evangelical piety and the stirrings of first love

memes.label: Rachel Ray's First Love-Letter
memes.description: The letter from Luke Rowan that formally opens the acknowledged courtship—"Rachel Ray's First Love-Letter"(016-001)—an event that transforms private feeling into documented commitment

memes.label: Religion
memes.description: "The role of religion—both institutional and personal—in shaping the moral world of Rachel Ray, from Mrs. Prime's evangelical severity to Mr. Comfort's comfortable Anglicanism"

memes.label: Society
memes.description: The stratified social world of a Victorian market town — how rank, respectability, and gentility govern the interactions, aspirations, and judgments of the novel's characters.

memes.label: St. Mary's Church
memes.description: The Anglican parish church of Baslehurst, presided over by Mr. Comfort and representing the moderate Anglican establishment against which the Evangelical severity of Mrs. Prime and Mr. Prong is measured

memes.label: Sunday Church Service
memes.description: The recurring weekly ritual of Sunday worship at Baslehurst Church, where the town assembles, social hierarchies are displayed, and Mrs. Prime's severity and Rachel's restlessness coexist in the same pew

memes.label: The Ball Invitation
memes.description: The contested social invitation to Mrs. Tappitt's ball — a domestic drama that crystallises the novel's central conflict between social ambition, religious scruple, and romantic opportunity.

memes.label: The Brewery Confrontation
memes.description: The climactic confrontation at Bungall & Tappitt's brewery in which Mr. Tappitt, "driven to forget himself," seizes a poker as a weapon against Luke Rowan—the dispute's nadir and the moment that makes reconciliation impossible

memes.label: The Courtship of Rachel Ray
memes.description: The extended courtship of Rachel Ray by Luke Rowan—spanning the stile encounter, the ball, the love-letter, and the long period of waiting and uncertainty before their eventual union

memes.label: The Gossip Campaign
memes.description: The campaign of malicious gossip orchestrated by Mrs. Tappitt against Rachel Ray in the aftermath of the ball, spread through Baslehurst by Miss Pucker and other willing conduits

memes.label: The Reconciliation
memes.description: The final reconciliation between Rachel Ray and Luke Rowan after the period of estrangement caused by the brewery dispute and Mrs. Prime's opposition—the novel's romantic resolution

memes.label: The Stile
memes.description: The stile in Baslehurst churchyard where Rachel Ray and Luke Rowan share their first private conversation, a threshold space between sacred ground and the lane that leads toward Bragg's End

memes.label: The Churchyard Stile Encounter
memes.description: The first private conversation between Rachel Ray and Luke Rowan at the stile in Baslehurst churchyard—the novel's romantic catalyst in which he calls her by her Christian name and she "had allowed his doing so to pass by without notice"(004-052)

memes.label: The Tea Visit
memes.description: The social call paid by Mrs. Butler Cornbury to Bragg's End, extending aristocratic patronage into the domestic space of the Ray cottage and lending social legitimacy to Rachel's position

memes.label: The Walks
memes.description: The ritual of walking — along the Cawston road, to church, and through the lanes — as the novel's primary mechanism for chance encounters, private conversations, and the quiet expression of romantic longing.

memes.label: The Wedding of Rachel Ray and Luke Rowan
memes.description: The offstage marriage of Rachel Ray and Luke Rowan that concludes the novel—never depicted directly, but implied as the natural and happy resolution of their courtship

memes.label: Three Tappitt Sisters
memes.description: The three daughters of Mr. Tappitt — Martha, Cherry, and the youngest — who collectively embody the domestic sphere of the brewery household in Baslehurst.

memes.label: Torquay
memes.description: A fashionable seaside resort on the Devonshire coast, representing the Tappitt family's dream of social aspiration and retirement from the brewery trade.

memes.label: Walter Cornbury
memes.description: The son of Squire Cornbury and husband of Mrs. Butler Cornbury, whose brief appearances at the ball and in the election subplot represent the benevolent potential of the landed gentry
